1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

4	/-!
5	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
6	
7	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
8	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
9	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
10	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
11	
12	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
13	-/
14	
15	universe u
16	
17	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
18	
19	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
20	
21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)
47	
48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _
64	
65	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
66	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
67	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
68	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
69	  ext Y
70	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
71	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
72	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
73	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
74	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
75	  rfl
76	
77	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
78	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
79	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
80	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
81	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
82	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
83	  rfl
84	
85	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
86	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
87	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
88	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
89	
90	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
91	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
92	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
93	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
94	  exact one_smul _ _
95	
96	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
97	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
98	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
99	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
100	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
101	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
102	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
103	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
104	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
105	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
106	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
107	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
108	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
109	
110	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
111	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
112	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
113	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
114	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
115	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
116	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
117	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
118	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
119	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
121	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
122	
123	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
124	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
125	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
126	  classical
127	  induction s using Finset.induction with
128	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
129	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
130	
131	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
132	
133	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
134	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
135	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
136	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
137	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
138	
139	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
140	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
141	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
142	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
143	
144	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
145	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
146	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
147	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
148	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
149	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
150	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
151	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
152	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
153	
154	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
155	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
156	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
157	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
158	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
159	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
160	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
161	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
162	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
163	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
164	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
165	
166	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
167	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
168	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
169	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
170	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
171	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
172	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
173	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
174	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
175	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
176	    congr 1
177	    funext i q
178	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
179	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
180	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
181	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
182	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
183	
184	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
185	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
186	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
187	  rw [matrixEnd]
188	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
189	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
190	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
191	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
192	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
193	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
194	    by_cases h : p = i
195	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
196	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
197	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]
198	
199	/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
200	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
201	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
202	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
203	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
204	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
205	  hom := matrixEnd M
206	  inv := matrixEnd N
207	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
208	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
209	
210	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
211	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
212	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl
213	
214	/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
215	forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
216	the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
217	the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
218	isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
219	lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
220	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
221	    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
222	    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
223	      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
224	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]
225	
226	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
227	
228	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
229	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
230	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
231	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
232	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
233	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
234	
235	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
236	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
237	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
238	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
239	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
240	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
241	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
242	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
243	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
244	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
245	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
246	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
247	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
248	
249	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
250	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
251	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
252	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
253	    (k : Fin d) :
254	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
255	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
256	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
257	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
258	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
259	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
260	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
261	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
262	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
263	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
264	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
265	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
266	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
267	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
268	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
269	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
270	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
271	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
272	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
273	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
274	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
275	  have lift_eq :
276	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
277	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
278	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
279	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
280	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
281	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
282	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
283	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
284	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
285	    by_cases hpk : p = k
286	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
287	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
288	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
289	  rfl
290	
291	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
292	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
293	of sheaves of modules. -/
294	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
295	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
296	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
297	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
298	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
299	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
300	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
301	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
302	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
303	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
304	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
305	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
306	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
307	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
308	
309	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
310	
311	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
312	
313	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
314	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
315	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction.
316	
317	Construction (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): the glued sheaf is built directly as a
318	categorical limit — an **equalizer of pushforwards** — rather than a hand-built presheaf of
319	compatible families. Concretely, `glue` forms the two parallel maps
320	`∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{i,j} (ιᵢ ≫ f_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)` (one leg the adjunction unit composed
321	with the pushforward-composition comparison, the other transported across the inverse
322	transition `(g_ij)⁻¹`), and takes their equalizer inside `Scheme.Modules D.glued`. The
323	self-identity (C1) and triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) hypotheses `_hC1`/`_hC2` on the
324	family `g` are NOT consumed in forming the equalizer object (the limit exists for any family
325	of transition maps); they are the descent conditions pinned down downstream when the
326	restriction isomorphisms are produced. The body below and the `_hC1`/`_hC2` signature are
327	complete (axiom-clean since iter-056). -/
328	
329	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
330	
331	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
332	
333	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
334	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
335	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
336	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
337	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
338	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
339	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
340	
341	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
342	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
343	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
344	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
345	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
346	
347	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
348	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
349	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
350	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
351	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
352	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
353	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
354	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
355	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
356	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
357	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
358	
359	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
360	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
361	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
362	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
363	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
364	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
365	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
366	
367	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
368	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
369	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
370	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
371	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
372	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
373	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
374	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
375	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
376	    Category.assoc]
377	
378	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
379	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
380	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
381	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
382	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
383	the module cocycle (C2). -/
384	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
385	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
386	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
387	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
388	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
389	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
390	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
391	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
392	
393	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
394	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
395	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
396	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
397	
398	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
399	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
400	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
401	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
402	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
403	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
404	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
405	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
406	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
407	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
408	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
409	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
410	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
411	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
412	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
413	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
414	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
415	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
416	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
417	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
418	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
419	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
420	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
421	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
422	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
423	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
424	    D.glued.Modules :=
425	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
426	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
427	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
428	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
429	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
430	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
431	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
432	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
433	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
434	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
435	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
436	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
437	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
438	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
439	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
440	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
441	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
442	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
443	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
444	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
445	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
446	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
447	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
448	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
450	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
451	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
452	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
453	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
454	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
455	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
456	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
457	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
458	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
459	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
460	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
461	  equalizer a b
462	
463	/-- **Lift into the glued sheaf** (`def:gr_modules_glueHom`-adjacent primitive): a family of
464	morphisms `k i : W ⟶ (ι_i)_* M_i` whose two overlap restrictions agree (the hypothesis
465	`hk`, stated against the two legs of the descent equalizer) lifts to a morphism
466	`W ⟶ glue D M g _ _`. This is `equalizer.lift` for the descent equalizer of pushforwards;
467	it is the vehicle by which the tautological quotient is assembled from the chart
468	quotients. Project-local. -/
469	noncomputable def glueLift (D : Scheme.GlueData)
470	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
471	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
472	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
473	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
474	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
475	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
476	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
477	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
478	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
479	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
480	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
481	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
482	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
483	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
484	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k))
485	    {W : D.glued.Modules}
486	    (k : ∀ i, W ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i))
487	    (hk : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
488	      k p.1 ≫
489	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
490	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
491	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
492	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))
493	        = k p.2 ≫
494	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
495	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
496	              (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
497	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
498	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
499	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
500	            ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
501	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
502	            (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
503	              rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
504	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1)))) :
505	    W ⟶ glue D M g _hC1 _hC2 :=
506	  equalizer.lift (Pi.lift k) (by
507	    apply Pi.hom_ext
508	    intro p
509	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
510	    exact hk p)
511	
512	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
513	
514	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
515	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
516	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
517	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
518	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
519	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
520	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
521	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
522	morphisms. -/
523	
524	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
525	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
526	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
527	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
528	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
529	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
530	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
531	  constructor
532	  intro V
533	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
534	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
535	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
536	  apply zigzag_isConnected
537	  intro s t
538	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
539	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
540	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
541	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
542	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
543	
544	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
545	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
546	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
547	Project-local. -/
548	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
549	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
550	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
551	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
552	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
553	
554	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
555	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
556	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
557	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
558	    eqToHom (congrArg
559	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
560	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
561	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
562	  subst h; simp
563	
564	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
565	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
566	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
567	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
568	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
569	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
570	    (I : Type u) :
571	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
572	      = eqToIso (congrArg
573	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
574	          h) := by
575	  subst h; simp
576	
577	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
578	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
579	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
580	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
581	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
582	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
583	Project-local. -/
584	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
585	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
586	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
587	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
588	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
589	  intro i
590	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
591	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
592	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
593	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
594	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
595	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
596	
597	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
598	
599	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
600	
601	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
602	
603	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
604	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
605	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
606	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
607	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
608	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
609	
610	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
611	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
612	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
613	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
614	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
615	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
616	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
617	
618	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
619	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
620	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
621	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
622	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
623	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
624	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
625	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
626	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
627	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
628	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
629	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
630	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
631	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
632	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
633	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
634	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
635	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
636	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
637	
638	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
639	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
640	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
641	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
642	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
643	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
644	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
645	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
646	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
647	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
648	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
649	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
650	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
651	    matrixToFreeIso
652	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
653	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
654	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
655	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
656	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
657	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
658	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
659	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
660	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
661	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
662	
663	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
664	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
665	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
666	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
667	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
668	
669	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
670	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
671	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
672	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
673	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
674	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
675	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
676	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
677	`chartTransition`. -/
678	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
679	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
680	      = eqToIso (congrArg
681	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
682	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
683	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
684	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
685	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
686	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
687	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
688	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
689	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
690	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
691	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
692	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
693	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
694	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
695	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
696	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
697	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
698	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
699	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
700	      = Iso.refl _ := by
701	    apply Iso.ext
702	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
703	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
704	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
705	  simp only [bundleTransition]
706	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
707	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)
708	
709	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
710	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
711	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
712	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
713	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
714	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
715	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
716	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
717	  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
718	
719	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)
720	
721	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
722	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
723	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
724	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
725	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
726	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/
727	
728	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
729	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
730	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
731	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
732	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
733	
734	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
735	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
736	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
737	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
738	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
739	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
740	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
741	
742	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_map_eq_of_comp` (private there). -/
743	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp' {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*}
744	    [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
745	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
746	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
747	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
748	
749	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left` (private there). -/
750	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
751	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
752	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
753	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
754	  rw [map_mul] at h
755	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
756	
757	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` (private there). -/
758	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
759	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
760	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
761	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
762	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
763	
764	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.imageMatrix_map_eq` (private there). -/
765	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
766	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
767	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
768	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
769	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
770	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
771	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
772	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
773	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
774	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
775	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
776	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
777	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
778	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
779	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
780	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
781	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
782	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
783	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
784	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
785	    ← map_nonsing_inv' incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
786	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
787	  congr 1
788	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
789	
790	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (private there): over the triple
791	overlap `S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied
792	entrywise to the image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. -/
793	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
794	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
795	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
796	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
797	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
798	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
799	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
800	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I K hI hK
801	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
